text: HRV-guided training is superior to predefined training for enhancing vagal-related HRV indices with a moderate effect size.
HRV-guided training does not significantly improve resting heart rate compared to predefined training.
HRV-guided training produces only a small, non-significant advantage over predefined training for improving maximal aerobic capacity (VO2max).
HRV-guided training produces only a small, non-significant advantage over predefined training for improving aerobic capacity at the second ventilatory threshold.
HRV-guided training produces only a small, non-significant advantage over predefined training for improving endurance performance.
HRV-guided training is less likely to produce negative autonomic responses (maladaptation) compared to predefined training programs.

Best practices for HRV-guided training regarding index selection, recording position, baseline reference values, and daily change thresholds (fixed vs. rolling averages) remain unestablished and require further study.
